category_title: Young children with persistent challenging behaviors across multiple routines
minimal_context: The student is in the childcare center and needs to put blocks in bins and line up on a floor shape for the cleanup routine.
dialogue:
TUTOR: This is the 1-minute warning signal before cleanup starts. We will need to put the blocks away and line up.

STUDENT: (Screams at the transition warning signal.)

TUTOR: It is time to clean up now. Please put the blocks in the bins.

STUDENT: (Cries and refuses to pick up the blocks.)

TUTOR: You need to put the toys on the shelves before you can line up on your floor shape.

STUDENT: (Throws the toys across the room and runs to stand on a floor shape to line up.)

options.A: Tell the student that running to the floor shape instead of cleaning up the blocks in the bins was a good idea.
options.B: Allow the student to withdraw from the cleanup routine and avoid putting the blocks in the bins before lining up on the floor shape.
options.C: Provide a gentle touch on the student's shoulder during the transition to the cleanup routine and lining up on the floor shape.
options.D: Use a First-Then statement combined with a visual schedule, saying, 'First clean up two blocks, then go line up,' while showing pictures of the steps.
